panels.description: Shows the Jaeger collector OpenTelemetry pipeline: receivers, processors, batch, and exporters, derived from otelcol_* metrics. Node and edge labels come from the dashboard queries; enable node/edge stats in the panel settings to show span rates on the graph

targets.expr:
label_join(
  label_join(
    rate(otelcol_receiver_accepted_spans_total{namespace=~"$namespace"}[$__rate_interval]),
    "id", "", "transport", "receiver"
  ),
  "title", "", "transport", "receiver"
)
or
label_replace(
  label_replace(
    sum by (service_name) (
      rate(otelcol_receiver_accepted_spans_total{namespace=~"$namespace"}[$__rate_interval])
    ),
    "id", "processor", "dummynode", ""
  ),
  "title", "processor", "dummynode", ""
)
or
label_replace(
  label_replace(
    rate(otelcol_processor_batch_batch_send_size_sum{namespace=~"$namespace"}[$__rate_interval]),
    "id", "$0", "processor", ".*"
  ),
  "title", "$0", "processor", ".*"
)
or
label_replace(
  label_replace(
    sum by (exporter) (
      rate(otelcol_exporter_sent_spans_total{namespace=~"$namespace"}[$__rate_interval])
    ),
    "id", "exporter", "dummynode", ""
  ),
  "title", "exporter", "dummynode", ""
)
or
label_replace(
  label_replace(
    sum by (exporter) (
      rate(otelcol_exporter_sent_spans_total{namespace=~"$namespace"}[$__rate_interval])
    ),
    "id", "$0", "exporter", ".*"
  ),
  "title", "$0", "exporter", ".*"
)

label_join(
  label_replace(
    label_join(
      rate(otelcol_receiver_accepted_spans_total{namespace=~"$namespace"}[$__rate_interval]),
      "source", "", "transport", "receiver"
    ),
    "target", "processor", "", ""
  ),
  "id", "-", "source", "target"
)

or

label_join(
  label_replace(
    label_replace(
      rate(otelcol_processor_batch_batch_send_size_sum{namespace=~"$namespace"}[$__rate_interval]),
      "source", "processor", "", ""
    ),
    "target", "$0", "processor", ".*"
  ),
  "id", "-", "source", "target"
)

or

label_join(
  label_replace(
    label_replace(
      rate(otelcol_processor_batch_batch_send_size_sum{namespace=~"$namespace"}[$__rate_interval]),
      "source", "$0", "processor", ".*"
    ),
    "target", "exporter", "", ""
  ),
  "id", "-", "source", "target"
)

or

label_join(
  label_replace(
    label_replace(
      rate(otelcol_exporter_sent_spans_total{namespace=~"$namespace"}[$__rate_interval]),
      "source", "exporter", "", ""
    ),
    "target", "$0", "exporter", ".*"
  ),
  "id", "-", "source", "target"
)

panels.title: OTel Collector pipeline map

panels.description: Number of unique services that sent spans to the collector during the selected dashboard time range
panels.title: Active Services Count

panels.description: Lists upstream services observed in span-derived metrics: current span ingestion rate and the total span count attributed to each service over the dashboard time range
panels.title: Trace-emitting services (rate & volume)

panels.description: Number of distinct metadata value combinations being processed
panels.title: Metadata cardinality

panels.description: Time series of per-service span ingestion rate, derived from spanmetrics counter traces_span_metrics_calls_total.
panels.title: Span observations per second by upstream service

panels.description: Aggregate rate of writes from Jaeger to the backing store (all tracestore tables combined)
panels.title: Attempts - inserts to storage, rate

panels.description: Rate of write attempts minus successful inserts per second. Values above zero indicate additional attempts (e.g. retries). Zero means attempts and inserts match in aggregate.
panels.title: Extra write attempts

panels.description: Fraction of spans that failed to be written to the backing store via the jaeger_storage_exporter, relative to successful plus failed export attempts. This reflects the collector’s view of storage write failures, not database-internal metrics.
panels.title: Span write failure ratio (collector to storage)

panels.description: 99th percentile latency of successful Jaeger storage operations to the backend (histogram jaeger_storage_latency_ok_seconds). Failed operations are not included. Grouping uses the table label. High values may indicate Cassandra, network, or load issues.
panels.title: Operation latency (P99, successful)

panels.description: Rate at which Jaeger skipped tag index updates (e.g. due to size or policy limits). Metric: jaeger_storage_tag_index_skipped_total.
panels.title: Tag index updates skipped (rate)
store latency for "duration_index" table, OK operations, seconds

store latency for "traces" table, ERROR operations, seconds
responses per rpc bucket for code "$grpc_status_code"
RPC server duration for "$grpc_status_code" gRPC status code
